display.purpose: The wordmark in the header; the one tracked-open moment.

title.purpose: Project card titles and section headings.

body.purpose: Descriptions and bio copy; cap measure at 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Tag pills and footer; small and quiet, never a tracked eyebrow.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: The single elevation token under cards and panels; shadow color is theme-bound (deep blue in the Console, soft rose in the Workshop).

motion.name: persona-morph
motion.purpose: The core mechanic: one progress value interpolates all eight theme variables as the slider moves.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: prefers-reduced-motion fallback: snap between persona states with a simple fade rather than removing the mechanic.

components.name: Project Card
components.description: Asymmetric two-column media + body card; the surface, border, and shadow all morph with the slider.
components.html:
Surfboard, shaped by hand

A 6'2" performance shortboard glassed in the garage over three weekends.

  • Foam
  • Resin
See the build
components.css: .ds-card { display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(0,5fr) minmax(0,6fr); gap: clamp(1.25rem,3vw,2.5rem); align-items: center; padding: 1.25rem; border-radius: 1.25rem; border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #f4d7d3); background: var(--color-surface, #fff); box-shadow: 0 18px 44px var(--color-shadow, rgba(151,71,89,0.18)); } .ds-card-media { border-radius: 0.9rem; aspect-ratio: 16/10; background: var(--color-border, #f4d7d3); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 0.5rem; font-size: 1.3rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: var(--color-text, #43262c); } .ds-card-desc { margin: 0 0 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: var(--color-text-muted, #92686f); } .ds-card-tags { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.5rem; list-style: none; margin: 0 0 1rem; padding: 0; } .ds-link { color: var(--color-accent, #e85d75); font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; } .ds-link:hover, .ds-link:focus-visible { text-decoration: underline; }

components.name: Tag Pill
components.description: Outlined full-pill tag; no fill, muted text, border morphs with the room.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-block; font-size: 0.78rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.7rem; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #f4d7d3); color: var(--color-text-muted, #92686f); }

components.name: CTA Link
components.description: The page's only call to action: a colored text link, no filled button. Accent morphs cyan ↔ coral.
components.css: .ds-link { display: inline-block; color: var(--color-accent, #e85d75); font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; transition: text-decoration-color 0.2s; } .ds-link:hover, .ds-link:focus-visible { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-link:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--color-accent, #e85d75); outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview: Two physical rooms bridged by a single slider. At progress 0 you stand in the Developer Console — a deep slate-navy room lit by screen-glow, cyan catching the edges, the calm precision of reading a stack trace at night. At progress 1 you step into the Workshop — a warm near-white space of coral light, foam dust, and shaped wood, where the same person builds surfboards and shelves by hand. Every position between is a real room: eight CSS custom properties interpolate continuously so the whole page recolors as one gesture. The system rejects the bootcamp dark-mode portfolio and the cream-and-eyebrow SaaS landing alike, and refuses to be twitchy — one motion centerpiece, everything else calm. Components feel tactile and precise: soft 20px radii, real depth, confident spacing.
keyCharacteristics: One progress value (0 → 1) drives all eight theme variables simultaneously.
Two fully resolved palettes; the live surface is whatever sits between them.
Tactile-precise components: 20px card radius, full-pill tags, real interpolated shadows.
One motion centerpiece (persona morph + slider); reveal/parallax stay quiet.
Single Avenir family carrying the whole system through weight and size.

rules.name: The Two-Rooms Rule
rules.body: Color is never chosen — it is read off the slider. Reference var(--color-*) so every component morphs; hardcoding a persona hex is a bug.

rules.name: The No-Gray Rule
rules.body: Muted text is a desaturated shade of the room's own hue (slate-blue / rose-brown), never neutral gray.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: No second typeface. Contrast comes from weight (400 ↔ 600) and size, never a new font.

rules.name: The Theme-Bound Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows use var(--color-shadow), never a hardcoded black rgba; the rose shadow belongs to the Workshop.

dos: Do reference var(--color-*) tokens for every color so components morph with the slider.
Do keep muted text a desaturated shade of the room's own hue, never gray.
Do carry hierarchy with weight and size in the single Avenir family.
Do use the theme-bound shadow for elevation, never a hardcoded black.
Do let the persona morph be the one motion centerpiece; keep reveal/parallax quiet.
Do keep both rooms equal — neither Engineer nor Human is a footnote.
donts: Don't build the generic dev portfolio: gradient hero blob, repo-card grid, skill-bar charts.
Don't drift toward the corporate SaaS landing: cream backgrounds, tracked uppercase eyebrows, big-number metric cards, stock 3D illustration.
Don't get gimmicky or over-animated: no cursor trails, no fade-in on every section, no parallax everywhere.
Don't collapse into sterile minimalism: black Helvetica on white with no point of view.
Don't hardcode a persona hex or use a black rgba shadow; both break the interpolation.
Don't introduce a second font family or all-caps tracked body copy.
